title: pelargonium × mathildinae
intro: Pelargonium × mathildinae, also known as Mountain Rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues, including digestive problems and colds. The health benefits of Pelargonium × mathildinae include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis. Pelargonium × mathildinae can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the tea being the most commonly used preparation, and is often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
